description: Fake Taiwan privacy regression fixtures. All values are obviously synthetic and do not correspond to any real person, patient, or customer. Use to prove sensitive-looking content is detected and cannot silently auto-promote. This is NOT medical compliance.

cases.content: Phone +886-0987-654-321 reached the clinic front desk.
cases.note: The built-in taiwan_mobile regex requires the leading 0 even after the +886 international prefix. Real-world international format drops the leading 0 (e.g. +886-987-654-321) and is NOT matched by taiwan_mobile; it is only caught by the generic phone pattern. This is a known limitation of the built-in pattern; broader clinic privacy profiles require a separate design discussion per issue #394.

cases.content: Patient fake ID C234567890 used only for testing the privacy gate.
ID Z256789012 is another fake test value for the privacy gate.

cases.note: Phone-like clinic record numbers are caught by the generic phone pattern; non-phone-like clinic record numbers are NOT caught by built-in patterns and require project-specific VAULT_PRIVACY_TERMS configuration.

cases.content: Clinic record CLINIC-REC-0001 is not detected by built-in patterns.
cases.note: Documents the false-negative gap. Projects must add their own clinic record-number patterns via VAULT_PRIVACY_TERMS or a project-local entity_rules.yaml; broader clinic privacy profiles require a separate design discussion per issue #394.
